title: Systems and methods for linking and storing biologics
abstract: Systems and methods for linking, tracking, storing, and/or accessing biological samples, such as biologics. In some embodiments, the system may comprise a kit including a biologics container marked with a readable code and a means for retrievably displaying the trackable code. The system may further comprise one or more storage containers for storing a plurality of samples and may be configured to link the code(s) with a specific storage location to provide for subsequent access to such samples by, for example, scanning or otherwise reading the code to identify the desired storage location.
claims:
Claims 1 A method comprising: (a) associating a unique identifier with a biological sample and one or more portions of the biological sample that is separated from the biological sample, wherein the unique identifier comprises updatable information to evaluate the biological sample or the one or more portions of the biological sample; and (b) employing a remote database or a cloud system to update the updatable information of the unique identifier. 2 The method of claim 1, wherein the unique identifier is associated with each of the one or more portions of the biological sample. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the unique identifier is associated with a second portion of the biological sample. 4. The method of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the unique identifier identifies a location or temperature of the biological sample or the one or more portions of the biological sample. 5. The method of any one of claims 1-4, wherein the updatable information is updatable from a remote location. 6 The method of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the updatable information is updated automatically. 7. The method of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the updatable information is updated manually. 8 The method of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the updatable information is stored in a database. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the database is a searchable database. 10 The method of claim 9, wherein the searchable database is searchable by a unique identifier, a genomic information, a proteomic information, an endosomic information, a medical history, genetically related biological samples, or any combination thereof. 11 The method of any one of claims 1-10, wherein the unique identifier correlates to a genomic information, a proteomic information, an endosomic information, a medical history, or any combination thereof. 12 The method of any one of claims 1-11, wherein the biological sample comprises a blood sample, a plasma sample, an extracellular fluid sample, a cell-free sample, a tissue sample, an isolated and purified cell, or any combination thereof. 13. The method of any one of claims 1-12, wherein the remote database or the cloud system instructs updating of the unique identifier with the updatable information following an event. 14. The method of any one of claims 1-13, wherein the remote database or the cloud system comprises more than one user portal. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein a user of the more than one user portal is a medical professional, a lab technician, a patient, or a regulatory compliance personal. 16. The method of claim 14 or 15, wherein a user accessing the remote database or the cloud system by one of the more than one user portals accesses the updatable information. 17. The method of any one of claims 1-16, further comprising tracking one or more events, wherein the one or more events comprises: packaging of a portion of the biological sample, administration of a portion of the biological sample or the one or more portions of the biological sample to a subject in need thereof, modification of a portion of the biological sample or the one or more portions of the biological sample, performing an analysis on the biological sample or the one or more portions of the biological sample, or any combination thereof. 18. The method of any one of claims 1-17, wherein the one or more portions of the biological sample is subjected to an analysis, and wherein the analysis is a genetic analysis, a proteomic analysis, a sequencing analysis, a cytological analysis, a kinetic analysis, a growth analysis, or any combination thereof. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein the analysis is the genetic analysis. 20. The method of any one of claims 1-19, wherein the unique identifier comprises a QR code. 21. The method of claim any one of claims 1-19, wherein the unique identifier comprises a barcode. 22. The method of any one of claims 1-21, wherein the unique identifier is scannable. 23. The method of any one of claims 1-22, wherein the biological sample or the one or more portions of the biological sample is prepared for a cellular-based or tissue-based therapy. 24. The method of any one of claims 1-23, wherein biological sample or the one or more portions of the biological sample is packaged or stored in a container and wherein the unique identifier is associated with the container. 25. The method of any one of claims 1-24, wherein the unique identifier identifies a subject, a subjectâs race, or a subjectâs sex from which the biological sample or the one or more portions of the biological sample is obtained. 26. The method of any one of claims 1-25, wherein the unique identifier comprises a medical history, a genetic information, a gender, an age, a smoking status, a cancer status, a hepatitis 

 status, an HIV status, a blood type, or any combination thereof of the subject from which the biological sample or the one or more portions of the biological sample was obtained. 27. The method of claim any one of claims 1-26, wherein the subject is an infant, an pregnant women, or a recently post-partum women. 28. The method of any one of claims 1-26, wherein the unique identifier identifies a related unique identifier associated with a subject that is genetically related to the subject from which the biological sample or the one or more portion of the biological sample is obtained. 29. The method of any one of claims 1-28, wherein the unique identifier identifies a subject from which the biological sample or the one or more portions of the biological sample is associated. 30. The method of any one of claims 1-29, wherein the subject is a subject receiving the biological sample or the one or more portions of the biological sample as part of a cellular- based or tissue based therapy. 31. The method of any one of claims 1-30, wherein the unique identifier identifies each portion of the one or more portions of the biological sample with at least 95% accuracy. 32. The method of any one of claims 1-31, wherein the biological sample comprises a stem cell. 33. The method of any one of claims 1-32, wherein the one or more portions of the biological sample comprise a stem cell. 34. The method of any one of claims 1-33, wherein the biological sample or the one or more portions of the biological sample is obtained from an umbilical cord tissue, a placenta tissue, a cord blood, or any combination thereof. 35. A wearable device comprising a unique identifier for a biological sample obtained from a subject, wherein the unique identifier comprises updatable information to evaluate the biological sample or any portion thereof, and wherein the unique identifier is in communication with a remote database or a cloud system to update the updatable information of the unique identifier. 36. The wearable device of claim 35, wherein the wearable device comprises a microchip. 37. The wearable device of claim 35-36, further comprising tracking one or more events, wherein the one or more events comprises: packaging a portion of the biological sample or the one or more portions of the biological sample, administration of a portion of the biological sample or the one or more portions of the biological sample to a subject in need thereof, modification a portion of the biological sample, performing an analysis on the biological sample or the one or more portions of biological sample, or any combination thereof. 38. The wearable device of claim 35-37, wherein a portion of the wearable device is scannable to automatically update the updatable information of the unique identifier.
